textByHashes.8d9cfc23f284d0fd1f630a397cb2f73ee119daba: Ah, hello Zanik, hello . How is the dig going?
textByHashes.395bc1d490e6894d0c8cf798ac719080aa3cbb67: Hmm... that is interesting. What do you think it is?
textByHashes.68ef6c3a528b59c74e1aa58f14bb41c603ebffc4: I can see how. I certainly wouldn't want to be hit with it!
textByHashes.696dd7e89fe5e75d744282eae6b9a51b747085a3: No, I'm afraid not. Our written language has developed so much since then that it's totally indecipherable to me.
textByHashes.13f2ba1a5fe5a8687a33ff231837aeb7e2c80d1f: Wait a moment. , didn't you say you talked to some surface goblins when you were trying to find your way through our mines?
textByHashes.d76b760cc2afd076a9a5948ec98b3f7c8c1685ac: Perhaps you could talk to them again. They might know more about weapons than we do.
textByHashes.8957dabe4a4a5dfeabcf6b03911a2fe61e794aee: Hello, Zanik. Have you found out about that mace yet?
textByHashes.5aa8262aad3f66ed04f76c1d473c382521b57dc8: I'm writing the definitive history of Dorgesh-Kaan!
textByHashes.f0e590e597c1af60b4a4e64012ed869d032169f4: Actually, I wonder if you could help me...
textByHashes.f61c5b1b71f24f53a9da71fd3f6deb7635b6fb79: I know the ruler of Lumbridge is called Horacio, but I don't know what his title is. Can you tell me?
textByHashes.6deaa249230fd474de5112701f3a2e33f99b8508: You've talked to the goblins on the surface, haven't you? Do you know the name of the god that the surface goblins worship?
textByHashes.1e4f9adc033fefc6adaf7a748b93a4185dae0bc8: I'm writing about the great war between the gods that happened thousands of years ago. Do you know the reason for this war?
textByHashes.14f8992e3ce15ad44abbcb013bdaa1af457f85f4: Just as I'd thought. Thank you.
textByHashes.1b96319625cc13ad264cce2738359e414d4ac8a0: Really? Thank you for the information.
textByHashes.2220fe8510d8824949573820234809620765fa7a: A strange idea of balance! Thank you.
textByHashes.81bab94de9befc2ad4a3d729a3afc883756694a9: What's the name of the group of surface-people that tried to destroy Dorgesh-Kaan?
textByHashes.b93b0d2f70a10bedd64a9e34d39bd3a7eeef6cfe: Hmm, I wonder what that stands for?
textByHashes.5d6bd3936dc79e9cc00a37b847d1dd6b391504a8: So we're monsters, are we? Thanks for telling me!
textByHashes.f94c7d59c8dca7107b752896c621a6c5aa30f0dd: Hmm, I wonder what that stands for. Thank you.
textByHashes.13cccdb62b50ca821aeaa7a4f777d587e2de1e51: Hmm, I wonder what that stands for? Thank you.
textByHashes.83f3877a7337c9c6fec874220175eacc662b7ab9: What species are you, if you don't mind my asking? I know there are many different races living on the surface, but I don't know which one you are.
textByHashes.06f86e6638fbafc3b7e5656204de87aab43ac12b: Ah, good, that's what I thought.
textByHashes.928bb0a3f1a22dadb8858829be47fd3b27843fa7: That's a relief! I thought you were some kind of horrible ogre!
textByHashes.c9ecac06be9a5d11918beff8cdd9290e040ccc77: Ah, of course. I thought you looked a little tall for a dwarf.
textByHashes.72dd1005ae831b752edf7b7023dfe4b7323076ae: Oh! I thought you were an elf. Surface species are confusing!
textByHashes.203e3cbf3228410956de2f8fa08b9d2dceba4303: Eek! An ogre! I had thought you were human!
textByHashes.b03b27c7604f2aa38d4ea6b80f12fb9246a69d69: And I thought you were an elf! What a silly mistake!
textByHashes.a0e225e8aa1aab89a3a645e1fe659bf8f83c9eed: Oh? I thought dwarves were shorter. I thought you were human!
textByHashes.8987cf0a654a928950d6f487c3a42c884fab4978: Ah, yes, of course, you said.
textByHashes.e613f51e45675d1760f1b2f3dca02b4189fb5f8a: Oh? I thought elves were more graceful. I thought you were human!
textByHashes.d55ff810149614dc342d2d467255681bd3613b49: Oh? I thought elves were more graceful. I thought you were a dwarf!
textByHashes.4fb675ea3eb082bb8ed180fda3fed4b6d429754d: Ah yes, that's what I thought.
textByHashes.328d454d49dbce2f41a5ebd78aafcb9aed9ef34a: This is the Dorgeshuun Council chamber!
textByHashes.46a32c8d694aa441a73a42505bb1f1ccb31a9477: Whenever a decision needs to be made, the seven members of the Dorgeshuun Council meet here. The last meeting was when they decided to open up the city to surface-dwellers.
textByHashes.078320b683e8bb190d69ea97cef048f5c7dd7359: In between meetings, I work here writing up the minutes of the meetings and working on the definitive history of Dorgesh-Kaan.
textByHashes.3ca68b278b550a5df9a6b637fcc98eefe9caa7e1: Of course! I've gone through all of my predecessors' notes and compiled the definitive history of the Dorgeshuun! What would you like to hear about?
textByHashes.3d5837aa2cecc84e76c0ca333b5a906c9974a846: Before Dorgesh-Kaan, the Dorgeshuun lived on the surface with many other goblin tribes. All goblins were ruled over by an evil god whose name has been forgotten.
textByHashes.db14d8f4ebf33890b397113669b363595ce25fa0: The gods became bored and decided to make the mortal races fight for their amusement. The goblins were sent to fight in many bloody battles against each other and other races.
textByHashes.b77e97e83312f1a755afcb46e6fd63a28898cce1: The war lasted for thousands of years, and millions of goblins died. Eventually, the evil god sent the Dorgeshuun tribe to fight a battle that they could not win.
textByHashes.93e31c40eda308b1d1535c309abc1b65d9481938: But General Bloodfist of the Dorgeshuun refused to fight in this battle. Instead, he commanded the Dorgeshuun army to march down into a fissure in the ground.
textByHashes.58417235cd3fe005ef5d3c8adc32e05f68a00094: General Bloodfist stood at the mouth of the fissure and defied the might of the evil god.
textByHashes.741958434ea9553e3829b6ffb9a3d2864c237b07: The evil god smote him with a mighty blast that shook the earth, but that blast also closed the fissure, sealing the Dorgeshuun away from the reach of the gods.
textByHashes.7458679904eab88b5c06ce858bcf229707a2422a: Lieutenant Strongaxe, now the leader of the Dorgeshuun, led the tribe deeper into the caves. They found this cavern, and here they founded Dorgesh-Kaan, city of the Dorgeshuun.
textByHashes.3690f436e55f28f30caea29e6247ad17279ae90c: The god Zamorak attacked the other gods, starting a great war. The goblins were sent to fight in many bloody battles against each other and other races.
textByHashes.f6bbbd90a8368e888c30ae88b74b748d97a1436a: The other gods decided to remove the great god Zaros from power, and started a great war. The goblins were sent to fight in many bloody battles against each other and other races.
textByHashes.76ff9dc11b292cde70ae1b6086d1d0a82e190896: In order to preserve balance, the god Guthix made the other gods fight against one another. The goblins were sent to fight in many bloody battles against each other and other races.
textByHashes.983cd64c2c4f3f32926d191744933ddfdb0484be: Before Dorgesh-Kaan, the Dorgeshuun lived on the surface with many other goblin tribes. All goblins were ruled over by an evil god called Big High War God.
textByHashes.00eed71675151da068fbd665863baf80881ec513: Before Dorgesh-Kaan, the Dorgeshuun lived on the surface with many other goblin tribes. All goblins were ruled over by an evil god called Zamorak.
textByHashes.d21a27daf70806799b3e3ff8e4e10f2054de9090: Before Dorgesh-Kaan, the Dorgeshuun lived on the surface with many other goblin tribes. All goblins were ruled over by an evil god called Guthix.
textByHashes.7d777f8857a4aca03c1009fb99f2a58757daa144: Before Dorgesh-Kaan, the Dorgeshuun lived on the surface with many other goblin tribes. All goblins were ruled over by an evil god called Saradomin.
textByHashes.1d46577787887cf6668996fea2f8c95b76111315: Before Dorgesh-Kaan, the Dorgeshuun lived on the surface with many other goblin tribes. All goblins were ruled over by an evil god called .
textByHashes.2d73932b16909ef00a2173a5e4de7a4b03eeb807: At first, Dorgesh-Kaan was ruled over by generals, just as the tribe had been when it was on the surface. Each general succeeded the last by defeating him in combat.
textByHashes.96ef46767c93c50d4bcf97cfd393114d8d338943: The generals were advised by a council of elders, but the Dorgeshuun were no longer an army, and many of the people felt that the Council should have authority.
textByHashes.43c527abfd6a7ff2f7e321aed78285dc1d0e9a17: The people voted to become a republic, but General Bonehelm refused to give up power, and so the Dorgeshuun Civil War began.
textByHashes.b85ca2a6e52bd71b875120388acc021cbfb09fa8: The republican faction was larger, but the military faction contained most of the trained warriors and controlled the magical equipment that the tribe had brought from the surface.
textByHashes.f940bf61d919985686accc726699eb2dc0293fa8: General Bonehelm was driven from the city, but dug his own base out of the caves nearby.
textByHashes.8143685e040f0e699fc8d638537997a95753e238: General Bonehelm's army attacked the city using archers mounted on giant frogs. There was a bloody battle, and General Bonehelm was driven back again into his base.
textByHashes.246aba107a438717aa3a21eee2be01ad9a7aa5fc: But General Bonehelm's mining had weakened the stone around his base, and the retreating army's footsteps caused the roof to collapse, crushing his army.
textByHashes.5f097d0d08a60cfe0d58585bf8f653962982ebf4: That was the last time one Dorgeshuun has raised arms against another. Since then, the city has been ruled peacefully by an elected council.
textByHashes.9f0929c4f4ccfe331278c769dfd512a02b41f789: Ah, this is where you come in! [clear throat]
textByHashes.3ce1982f980f6d4ad437ce26677e842edbf36371: In the twenty-ninth century since the founding of the city, one of the miners broke through a wall into the cellar of a surface dwelling. The miners blocked up the tunnel and went back into their mines.
textByHashes.3b24ca8fbed74509119f9874b16ff2bbd945ce0a: However, a human named found his way through the caves and made contact with the miners.
textByHashes.1dbcc3409c75747858e73f473fa0b9690f0dd977: However, a human named found her way through the caves and made contact with the miners.
textByHashes.f659bcbfb68ff0e333f52c87d86d018b33ed3077: However, a human named found their way through the caves and made contact with the miners.
textByHashes.ed868007b58d6cb8e66223a8981356d7c256b34e: Ur-tag, head of the Council, signed a peace treaty with Duke Horacio of Lumbridge, ruler of the town above the Dorgeshuuns' mine.
textByHashes.3f9764837d6ffb947213fa78be5ce38338e91da8: But not all surface-dwellers were friendly, and a faction called H.A.M. attempted to destroy the Dorgeshuun.
textByHashes.ffb148d9de9861e76c95ce3ac1bf6a4031a6316b: The Council sent Zanik to explore the surface to see if the H.A.M. group was plotting anything. With 's help, Zanik defeated a H.A.M. plot to flood Dorgesh-Kaan.
textByHashes.82ba4c7141a3f310016228bb9f2a02646c843630: Despite reservations from many citizens, the Council eventually voted 4-3 to open Dorgesh-Kaan to surface-dwellers.
textByHashes.65eb6fa526db1248e6d3ca395541a54786fab8c8: But not all surface-dwellers were friendly, and a faction called B.A.C.O.N. attempted to destroy the Dorgeshuun.
textByHashes.85efeda6228f3de13b395cfa76c13590f902784f: The Council sent Zanik to explore the surface to see if the B.A.C.O.N. group was plotting anything. With 's help, Zanik defeated a B.A.C.O.N. plot to flood Dorgesh-Kaan.
textByHashes.11f6452d14f4848d22ac3243e68ef3176f0d6005: But not all surface-dwellers were friendly, and a faction called P.O.R.K. attempted to destroy the Dorgeshuun.
textByHashes.cff896e211537c9e280228cc94fdaab0a95acb0c: The Council sent Zanik to explore the surface to see if the P.O.R.K. group was plotting anything. With 's help, Zanik defeated a P.O.R.K. plot to flood Dorgesh-Kaan.
textByHashes.7ec1e0eb8a74a3c94a90ab3fc00855b0fa9accdb: But not all surface-dwellers were friendly, and a faction called B.E.E.F. attempted to destroy the Dorgeshuun.
textByHashes.97bb8f528aba8c30956d084acd15f440fa81e1d8: The Council sent Zanik to explore the surface to see if the B.E.E.F. group was plotting anything. With 's help, Zanik defeated a B.E.E.F. plot to flood Dorgesh-Kaan.
textByHashes.791b9a8aaf8a2b4cb4a4b45c00212b7407f348d8: Ur-tag, head of the Council, signed a peace treaty with General Horacio of Lumbridge, ruler of the town above the Dorgeshuuns' mine.
textByHashes.efa9e03c6bae51076e9a568d513225fef4884778: Ur-tag, head of the Council, signed a peace treaty with King Horacio of Lumbridge, ruler of the town above the Dorgeshuuns' mine.
textByHashes.44f0b2d2907c8bf63dba6394d9ca9a2765b743a7: Ur-tag, head of the Council, signed a peace treaty with Lord Horacio of Lumbridge, ruler of the town above the Dorgeshuuns' mine.
textByHashes.acce5089be390f70ae23d7534ee76dc4640add3d: However, an ogre named found his way through the caves and made contact with the miners.
textByHashes.a61b5e922a97f7faa557754bc034e3336ab54efb: However, an ogre named found her way through the caves and made contact with the miners.
textByHashes.9dcb69bd8416af43da7579516a46b1689a23b8c0: However, an ogre named found their way through the caves and made contact with the miners.
textByHashes.5d88607ed29d116bd186bdd07829f0e6304611a9: However, a dwarf named found his way through the caves and made contact with the miners.
textByHashes.3ba2ada22effe945e28b129b12a848a55106ef5a: However, a dwarf named found her way through the caves and made contact with the miners.
textByHashes.452ab639a323534b0d1b0a7c29a5fc68e12c8510: However, a dwarf named found their way through the caves and made contact with the miners.
textByHashes.d88cc0fc10bac71cea9e612d655ded07a5b30b9f: However, an elf named found his way through the caves and made contact with the miners.
textByHashes.3f9af7aabb911347e2073dc4ff0a63fa749f70e4: However, an elf named found her way through the caves and made contact with the miners.
textByHashes.8e0cb8461a0480cf9e84859827011045b6f8ee8c: However, an elf named found their way through the caves and made contact with the miners.
textByHashes.caa6e78acfe35e692e99daf1214e3a1f89aebd98: Tell me again about the surface. You said the cavern ceiling was how high?
textByHashes.08e9f9a53d93b537fa99aeb088b448df2d9b3c8c: You couldn't see how high the ceiling was because the sky was in the way. I see.
textByHashes.44317aca4cf47aaa59b7bb83291e7245480f7898: I'm still having trouble getting my head around this 'surface' idea.
textByHashes.a487bc9a1235125bbe3160fb2c34260e48e92379: Oh, no, no, no. I think I'd rather stay here and write about it.
